transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Sunday, June 28. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, across india, around the world, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. 'Will be forced to militarily complete the job': Trump warns Iran after US strikes over 2nd ceasefire breach. The US launched fresh strikes on Iran after accusing Tehran of violating a ceasefire for a second consecutive day by attacking a commercial oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. This follows US retaliation for a drone attack on another vessel. Iran has warned of a "practical response" to the US actions, escalating regional tensions despite ongoing diplomatic efforts. 'A great example for Vaibhav': Ex-India captain urges Sooryavanshi to follow Sachin. Former captain Dilip Vengsarkar believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has a bright international future, urging him to emulate Sachin Tendulkar's discipline and focus. Despite not debuting against Ireland yet, Sooryavanshi's IPL success has drawn comparisons to Tendulkar. Vengsarkar is confident the young batter will seize his opportunity, highlighting his immense potential and unique style, though cautioning that his Test capabilities remain to be seen. Jeff Bezos versus Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O'Leary says Amazon founder should run cities. Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani files motion to ban delivery app Motoclick: What lawsuit says. New York City is seeking to ban food delivery platform Motoclick for allegedly paying workers as little as $3.67 per hour, a fraction of the mandated $22.13 minimum wage. City officials filed a motion for a preliminary injunction, aiming to halt the company's operations unless it complies with labor laws. 'Never hid information on Op Sindoor deaths': Government rejects misleading social media claims. NEW DELHI: : The defence ministry Saturday asserted that information about the deaths of six military personnel during the May 7-10 2025 Operation Sindoor was never hidden from the public, pointing out that in the press briefing held a day after operations were paused against Pakistan, the then Director General of Military Operations had mentioned the loss of the lives of the armed forces personnel and civilians. Supreme Court takes 14 years on criminal case, 2 convicts die waiting. NEW DELHI: Even as Supreme Court has repeatedly invoked the legal maxim that 'justice delayed is justice denied' , with CJI Surya Kant recently saying "justice delayed is justice destroyed" , the apex court has, on many occasions, taken more time than trial courts and HCs to decide a case. Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message. Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97 percent of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool. Monsoon on the back foot: IMD warns of over 10 percent rainfall deficit. NEW DELHI: Rainfall deficit of 43 percent as on Saturday will be reduced once the monsoon covers the entire country next month but the season, already affected by El Niño, may not get the climatic cushion of Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) to offset the impact this year. Venezuela jolted again: 5.6-magnitude earthquake strikes Aragua. Venezuela is grappling with a fresh 5.6 magnitude earthquake, compounding the devastation from earlier twin quakes that claimed over 1,430 lives. Rescue efforts are ongoing amidst widespread destruction, with thousands injured and homeless. International aid is arriving as authorities work to restore power and address critical shortages in the hardest-hit areas, while the UN estimates significant economic losses. 7 India-bound cargo ships cross Hormuz in 3 days, 15 in queue. NEW DELHI: Nine India- and foreign flagged ships have crossed the Strait of Hormuz in the past 72 hours, and seven of these are carrying India-bound cargo. PM Modi govt's silence on Gaza cost India morally, strategically: Sonia Gandhi. NEW DELHI: Congress neta Sonia Gandhi said Modi govt's silence on Gaza genocide has cost India both in morality and strategic interest without yielding anything for the country except friendship between Israel PM Benjamin Netanyahu and PM Modi. June 30 deadline nears for pre-2025 waqf properties to get legal status. NEW DELHI: The time for earning the legal waqf tag for properties that existed before the Waqf Amendment Act 2025 came into effect is running out fast.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. Axes On Bhoj Wetland, Encroachment Fears Grow. According to environment expert and senior botanist Mahesh Gupta, trees in the catchment area play a vital role in groundwater recharge, preventing soil erosion and reducing silt flow into the lake. Tower Thefts, Delays Slow BSNL 4G Expansion In State; 479 Sites Await Approval. Several other districts have also reported thefts, raising concerns over disruption of telecom services and delays in network expansion. Bhopal Municipal Corporation's Neglect Of Taxpayers: Nearly 1,500 Km Of Roads In Disrepair. During the review meeting, the divisional commissioner assessed the functioning of municipal departments and the progress of key development projects. Express Special From Ganga to global pet markets: Police in Madhya Pradesh map rail route of protected turtles. Four killed, eight injured in lightning strikes in Chhindwara, Madhya Pradesh. Congress holds 'Loktantra Bachao Satyagraha' in Bhopal over Rajya Sabha nomination rejection. Bhopal murder accused found HIV-positive; police trace contacts. In A First, 11 IIT-I Students Get Over rupees 1 Crore Package. Continuing his address, Joshi said that over the years, the IIT Indore community had published 10,700 papers in international journals, developed 280 technologies, executed 900 sponsored projects and 730 consultancy projects. Bhopal to host Global Investors Summit in 2027: CM. Madhya Pradesh CM Dr. Mohan Yadav congratulated Mohan Nagar and Bhagwan Das Raikwar on being conferred Padma Shri award Akashvani News. Vijayvargiya says, 'officials now claim RSS links': Minister remarks many officers began identifying with o. Madhya Pradesh judge gets death threats after mob lynching verdict. Man attacks live-in partner and her sister with sharp weapon in Bhopal. Boy abducts, rapes 15-year-old girl. Cricket match squads RNE versus CG, Final, Madhya Pradesh Premier League 2026. Satellites to stand guard over city's 32k acres of govt land. President Droupadi Murmu prays at Omkareshwar and Mamleshwar temples in Madhya Pradesh. Larger percentage of women compared to men support UCC introduction in Madhya Pradesh. CMRS Team Conducted Safety And Signalling System Of Bhopal Metro. Centre Pulls Plug On IIT Indore's Ujjain Satellite Campus. Sources in the state's technical education department confirmed that the satellite campus proposal has been dropped, with no possibility of revival in the foreseeable future.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. Rain deficit now at 43 percent , likely to end at over 10 percent. NEW DELHI: Rainfall deficit of 43 percent as on Saturday will be reduced once the monsoon covers the entire country next month but the season, already affected by El Niño, may not get the climatic cushion of Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD) to offset the impact this year. Never hid information on Operation Sindoor deaths: Government. NEW DELHI: : The defence ministry Saturday asserted that information about the deaths of six military personnel during the May 7-10 2025 Operation Sindoor was never hidden from the public, pointing out that in the press briefing held a day after operations were paused against Pakistan, the then Director General of Military Operations had mentioned the loss of the lives of the armed forces personnel and civilians. PM Modi govt's silence on Gaza cost India morally, strategically, says Sonia Gandhi. NEW DELHI: Congress neta Sonia Gandhi said Modi govt's silence on Gaza genocide has cost India both in morality and strategic interest without yielding anything for the country except friendship between Israel PM Benjamin Netanyahu and PM Modi. 1.2 lakh waqf assets under stages of verification. NEW DELHI: The time for earning the legal waqf tag for properties that existed before the Waqf Amendment Act 2025 came into effect is running out fast. TMC versus TMC: Mamata loyalist lodges police complaints against rebel MLAs for using party symbol. A political rift deepens within the Trinamool Congress as loyalist Dola Sen filed police complaints against rebel MLAs for alleged forgery and impersonation. The breakaway faction, led by Ritabrata Banerjee, met with former Kolkata councillors to bolster their support base. Rebel MLA Sandipan Saha asserted their legitimacy, claiming to be the "real Trinamool Congress. Indian passport holders abroad treated as citizens for using RTI. NEW DELHI: Amid the ongoing debate on whether passport is a conclusive proof of citizenship or just a travel document, records of interministerial deliberations in 2010 over who can avail of Right to Information Act, show that MEA and MHA agreed that holders of Indian passports residing abroad can be treated as "citizens" for the purpose of availing of the benefits of information law. Courts can examine the correctness of answer keys, says Delhi high court. NEW DELHI: Delhi high court has asked Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) to hear afresh a plea by a UPSC exam aspirant who challenged the correctness of answer keys to two questions. CGHS officials get financial powers to cut claim delays. NEW DELHI: Lakhs of central govt health scheme (CGHS) beneficiaries could soon spend less time waiting for approval of costly medical claims, surgeries and specialised treatment, with the Union health ministry increasing the financial powers of CGHS officials to clear more cases without escalating them further. 'We're working on for first uncrewed Gaganyaan mission this year', says Isro chief V Narayanan. India News: NEW DELHI: Isro chairman V Narayanan said on Saturday that the country is preparing for the first uncrewed Gaganyaan mission, advancing Chandrayaan-4,. 'Old' explosive kills civilian in Baramulla. SRINAGAR: A civilian was killed Saturday after coming into contact with an "old explosive object" that went off in north Kashmir's Baramulla district. 'As long as BJP is in power, paper leaks will persist': Akhilesh Yadav attacks Centre over exam irregularities. #WATCH Azamgarh, Uttar Pradesh: Samajwadi Party Chief Akhilesh Yadav says, "This is nothing new under this government. 'Even if they are hiding in depths of hell': Suvendu directs CID to ensure speedy custodial trial in alleged murder of BJP worker. NEW DELHI: West Bengal chief minister and BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari on Saturday directed the state Criminal Investigation Department (CID) to ensure a speedy custodial trial into the alleged murder of BJP worker Prashanta Dey. Sikh body backs Akal Takht's decision to brand Punjab CM 'Guru Dokhi' over 'sacrilege' row; Panthic meeting on July 5. NEW DELHI: The Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee (SGPC) on Thursday endorsed the Akal Takht Sahib's declaration of Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann as 'Guru Dokhi' (anti-Gur) and 'Khalsa Panth Virodhi' (anti-Khalsa Panth), while announcing a large Panthic gathering on 5 July to mobilise support for the directive and initiate legal action against the Aam Aadmi Party leader. 'Operation Devendra': Uddhav Thackeray says Amit Shah planned Sena (UBT) MP defections to 'sideline' Fadnavis. NEW DELHI: Shiv Sena (UBT) president Uddhav Thackeray on Saturday alleged that the BJP's so-called "Operation Tiger", the defection of six Lok Sabha MPs from his faction to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena earlier this week, was aimed at keeping Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, a BJP member, "in check." Bangladesh-China Joint Statement, Mongla Port's Implications For India Decoded. Teesta River Comprehensive Management and Restoration Project is being closely watched in India The Subtext Behind Congress's New UP Incharge On "Equal" Seat-Sharing. Formal talks regarding seat-sharing for the UP assembly elections have not yet begun Delhi-NCR Earthquake Highlights: 6.2 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Afghanistan, Tremors Felt In Delhi. Delhi-NCR Earthquake Highlights: Strong earthquake in Afghanistan, tremors felt in Delhi Father Lynches Two Children After Fight With Wife In Tripura. The accused allegedly attacked the two children with a wooden log. CBI Attaches rupees 53 Crore Assets Of Former Assam Top Cop In Corruption Case. The attached properties include multiple hotels in Guwahati and two buildings in Mumbai. Manipur Civil Groups Seek Action After Kuki Body Apologises For Killing 6 Naga Hostages. United Naga Council (UNC) members at a press conference in Delhi on Saturday 'Shortage Of Good People In RSS': BJP's Kailash Vijayvargiya Triggers Row. Kailash Vijayvargiya is Madhya Pradesh's urban development and housing minister.

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And here is what is happening around the world. Venezuela earthquakes kill 920 people as international rescue teams arrive. Hundreds are still feared trapped under the rubble, as families desperate for news. Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal. Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement. Trump's face is added to select US passports for America's 250th birthday. The commemorative special edition passports will be available on 6 July, and can only be applied for in person Religion row as Texas makes Bible stories required reading in schools. Critics say the new reading requirements infringe on religious freedoms and blur the separation of church and state. US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship. Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response. Burkina Faso severs diplomatic ties with France. The West African country's junta accused the former colonial power of undermining its national interests. Billionaire Leon Black walks out of Epstein investigation hearing. The House panel then issued subpoenas demanding the private equity tycoon, who co-founded Apollo Global Management, testify on camera and under oath. Pete Buttigieg briefly separated from children after false police report. Buttigieg says the day he spent separated from his twins was "among the darkest hours of my life". Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents. Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say. DR Congo takes Rwanda to international court over decades of conflict. Kinshasa accuses its neighbour of committing various violations since the 1994 Rwanda genocide. Mother dies saving daughter in Venezuela earthquakes. Héctor Bello wrote on social media that "you gave your own life for our daughter" in the quakes which killed at least 920 people. In Caracas, this feels like the hardest moment in Venezuela's modern history. Rescue teams are working ceaselessly to reach those trapped under rubble. But as hope fades, anger is growing. They quit the West for Russia's traditional values, but it wasn't what they expected. The BBC asks Westerners who moved to Russia if life there lives up to expectations. Could you handle a 20-plus hour flight? This airline is banking on it. Some analysts say the first non-stop London to Sydney flight is a major milestone - but would you buy a ticket? Hit South African show gets the world talking about polygamy and cheating. The Zulu-language series about a man with multiple wives is a worldwide hit. GTA 6 will launch as download only - does that mean the disc is dead?. Music and films are now largely digital-only - does the lack of a disc for GTA 6 mean gaming is going the same way? Just how much trouble is Canada's economy in?. Five charts help illustrate the state of Canada's economy - and how it's performing compared to other wealthy nations. Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty. The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces. The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet. Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work? Panic attacks and fractures - Venezuela hospital treats earthquake victims. The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. Turtlemint Fintech IPO GMP signals discounted market debut on 29 June; check subscription and other details. Turtlemint's IPO, open from June 19 to 21, was subscribed 0.68 times. It aims to grow its tech capabilities and brand presence with the raised funds. Established in 2015, Turtlemint connects customers, advisors, and insurers through a hybrid model. Vijay Kedia explains why every bull market ends the same way, and where the next opportunity could be. Vijay Kedia shares insights on market cycles, explaining that investment opportunities rotate through phases of optimism, euphoria, and correction. He encourages investors to recognize patterns in asset classes and advises focusing on long-term strategies rather than short-term trends. After months of pain, Dalal Street is ready for a comeback? ICICI Securities explains why. ICICI Securities reports that Indian equities are starting to improve due to better macroeconomic conditions, more attractive valuations, and reduced foreign selling. Despite previous market challenges, the outlook for equities is becoming more favorable as various risks begin to stabilize. US stock markets: Dow Jones hits record high, Nasdaq sinks 4.6 percent - What shook Wall Street this week?. For the week ended June 26, the Dow Jones Industrial Average outperformed its peers, gaining about 0.6 percent to close at a record high. The broader S and P 500, however, declined 2.0 percent , while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite tumbled 4.6 percent . Stocks to buy below rupees 100: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell. Next week, Bank Nifty's trading range is set between 57,000 and 59,000, with breakouts suggesting future trends. Analyst Mehul Kothari advises buying Trident, UCO Bank, and MMTC under rupees 100, as the Indian market remains strong amid mixed signals and easing crude oil prices. Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 29 June 2026. Sumeet Bagadia recommended these three buy-or-sell stocks: Dr Reddy's Labs, M and M, and Tata Consumer. Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold extends rebound above $4,100; silver gains for second day as dollar weakens. Gold and silver prices increased for the second day, aided by a weaker US dollar. Gold rose to $4,111, despite continuing a decline, while silver reached $59.53. Both metals are on track for significant weekly losses as gold's June decline reaches 11 percent . Crude oil prices tumble 5 percent as Strait of Hormuz traffic rebounds; Brent plunges 20 percent in June. Brent crude prices plummeted to $72 as supply concerns eased with resumed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The weekly decline reaches 10 percent , while June losses hit 20 percent , reflecting the impact of improved stability in Middle Eastern oil exports. India's ethanol strategy goes against the grain. With the rollout of E85 fuel and policy changes to support mass flex-fuel deployment, the government is attempting something few large economies have tried, decarbonising road transport while extending the life of the internal combustion engine (ICE). Can an OpenAI IPO delay derail the AI chip rally? Here's why it matters. A potential delay in OpenAI's IPO has triggered selling in global technology stocks, impacting major players like SoftBank. As concerns over AI investment returns grow, investors are increasingly selective in their stock choices, marking a shift in sentiment within the technology sector. Ram Temple Trust rocked over donation row. The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has pledged a thorough investigation into alleged donation embezzlement, confirming the resignations of General Secretary Champat Rai and Trustee Anil Mishra. BEML shares to be in focus on Monday after securing additional $5.35 million Middle East export order. BEML Ltd. secured a $5.35 million export order, raising its contract total to $41.73 million. The company's international order book reached $112.35 million. 'India GDP growth may ease fiscal stress'. India's fiscal deficit is expected to remain under control in FY27, thanks to robust nominal GDP growth driven by higher inflation, according to EY India. Goldman sees big tech stocks turning attractive amid chip volatility, 'you want to diversify toward hyperscalers'. Big tech stocks could turn more attractive in the artificial intelligence trade as chipmakers show continued volatility, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategist Christian Mueller India opens world's first nuclear heat H2 plant. Homebuyer paid for a flat in a Delhi housing society in 2003, got delayed possession, sought compensation for delay; SC defends his right. The SC said that the homebuyer can make a claim for compensation for delayed possession. "The subsequent receipt of possession cannot, by itself, extinguish the right of the allottee to seek adjudication of a claim for compensation for the alleged delay," the top court stated. The top court has also revived the 2005 complaint made by the homebuyer. Mumbai couple makes rupees 2.2 lakh a month yet can't save: Expert explains what they could do differently. Experts estimate substantial payouts for Level 1–5 employees if implementation is delayed by 20 months, with arrears ranging from about rupees 3.6 lakh to rupees 10.8 lakh under fitment factors between 2.0 and 2.86. ITR filing: Transferring investments to your spouse to save taxes is a common misconception. The Income Tax Department scrutinises fund sources and can club income back to the original provider. This applies to FDs, shares, and gold, where income or capital gains from gifted assets are often taxed in the transferor's hands. The analysis shows gold has significantly outperformed the Nifty 50 over the short term, particularly in the last one to five years. Over 10 years, both gold and the Nifty 50 have delivered broadly similar long-term returns. The Bombay High Court has ruled in favor of homebuyers, allowing them to form a single housing society for a multi-tower project. The court rejected a builder's plea to create separate societies, citing a nine-year delay in the builder's own society formation process. This landmark decision empowers residents to unite and manage their shared amenities effectively, ensuring their collective interests are protected against prolonged developer inaction. transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. Soaring iPad, Xbox prices reveal pain of memory chip mess. Apple and Microsoft increased prices for Xbox consoles, Macs, and iPads due to a memory chip shortage driven by AI demand. The Moto Pad 70 Pro features a 13-inch 3.5K display, Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor, and quad speaker setup. OpenAI has appointed Prabhjeet Singh as Managing Director for India. The company has also announced a limited release of its GPT-5.6 model, which features three variants: Sol, Terra, and Tuna iPhone 18 to get a big RAM upgrade as Apple doubles down on AI: Report. Apple is set to launch the iPhone 18 and iPhone 18e next spring with 9GB and the A20 chipset. The iPhone 18 lineup could also include improved AI features and brighter display, and better optics. India's robotics funding doubles in 2026 first-half, but scale seems far away. India's robotics sector continues to lag its global peers. In 2025, Indian robotics startups raised less than 1 percent of the capital secured by US robotics startups and about 2 percent of that raised in China, according to Tracxn. iPhone Ultra launch back on track after Apple fixes critical foldable issue: Report. Apple's foldable iPhone is set for a September launch after resolving hinge issues, as per a new report Apple price hike: Check updated prices of MacBook Neo, Mac mini M4, iPads and HomePod. Apple has raised prices for its MacBooks, iPads, and other products in India due to increasing memory chip costs. Apple raises MacBook and iPad prices amid memory chip crunch , Check new prices here. Memory chip makers such as Micron have, in recent months, prioritised orders from AI chipmakers like Nvidia, helping them earn record profits but leaving little supply for electronics makers that have been forced to increase prices.
